questions.text: The length and radius of an electric resistance of a certain wire are doubled simultaneously, then the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Resistance will be doubled and specific resistance will be halved
options.explanation: Doubling the length and radius (L and r) will result in a factor of 4 increase in the cross-sectional area (A).As a result, the resistance will remain unchanged if both the length and radius are doubled

options.text: Resistance will be halved and specific resistance will remain uncharged
options.explanation: The formula for resistance of wire is given by:ρ = RA/l.In Specific resistance there will be no Change .Specific resistance will remain the same as it is a material property but a point to be remembered is that it depends upon temperature.

options.text: Resistance will be halved and specific resistance will be doubled
options.explanation: Doubling the specific resistance (ρ) will directly lead to doubling the resistance, assuming all other factors remain constant.

options.text: Resistance and specific resistance will both remain uncharged
options.explanation: This scenario is not consistent with the physical relationship described in the formula. Doubling both length and radius would increase the cross-sectional area, affecting the resistance.

questions.explanation: Correct option is B. The formula for resistance of wire is given by: ρ = RA/l. In Specific resistance there will be no Change . Specific resistance will remain the same as it is a material property but a point to be remembered is that it depends upon temperature.

questions.text: A neon flashlight cell with an emf of 1.5V gives a current of 15A when connected directly to an ammeter of resistance 0.040 Ohms. The internal resistance of the cell is?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect emf= 1.5 VI = 15 AThe resistance of the ammeter in the circuit is = R = 0.04-ohm I = ε / R + r (r is the internal resistance of the cell)15 = 1.5 / r + 0.04r + 0.04 = 1.5 / 15r + 0.04 = 0.1r = 0.1 - 0.04r = 0.06 ohm.

emf= 1.5 VI = 15 AThe resistance of the ammeter in the circuit is = R = 0.04-ohm I = ε / R + r (r is the internal resistance of the cell)15 = 1.5 / r + 0.04r + 0.04 = 1.5 / 15r + 0.04 = 0.1r = 0.1 - 0.04r = 0.06 ohm.This is numerical so it can only have one answer so option B is correct

Incorrect emf= 1.5 VI = 15 AThe resistance of the ammeter in the circuit is = R = 0.04-ohm I = ε / R + r (r is the internal resistance of the cell)15 = 1.5 / r + 0.04r + 0.04 = 1.5 / 15r + 0.04 = 0.1r = 0.1 - 0.04r = 0.06 ohm.

Incorrect emf= 1.5 VI = 15 AThe resistance of the ammeter in the circuit is = R = 0.04-ohm I = ε / R + r (r is the internal resistance of the cell)15 = 1.5 / r + 0.04r + 0.04 = 1.5 / 15r + 0.04 = 0.1r = 0.1 - 0.04r = 0.06 ohm.

questions.explanation: emf= 1.5 VI = 15 AThe resistance of the ammeter in the circuit is = R = 0.04-ohm I = ε / R + r (r is the internal resistance of the cell)15 = 1.5 / r + 0.04r + 0.04 = 1.5 / 15r + 0.04 = 0.1r = 0.1 - 0.04r = 0.06 ohm.

questions.text: When the length of the conductor is doubled and the area of cross-section remains the same then its resistance
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect because L and R have a direct relation as indicated in formula
correct because L and R have direct relation with each other increasing R also increases L.
incorrect because R and L have a direct relation not indirect.
incorrect because they have a direct relation not a double direct relation.

questions.explanation: The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area. The resistance of a conductor, or circuit element, generally increases with increasing length. And decreasing area.

questions.text: When 2Ω, 4Ω and 6Ω resistor are connected in parallel their resultant equivalent resistance will be?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. This would be the equivalent resistance if they were connected in series, not parallel.
Incorrect. This is the reciprocal of the correct answer, not the equivalent resistance.
Given that R1= 2Ω, R2= 4Ω and R3 = 6Ω In parallel combinations Resistance is given as = 1/R1+ 1/R2+1/R3 ½+ ¼ +⅙ = 6/12 + 3/12 + 2/12 = 11/12 Since 1/R = 11/ 12 so Req will be equal to 12/11
Incorrect. There is an exact equivalent resistance for these resistors in parallel.

questions.explanation: To find the equivalent resistance of resistors in parallel, we use the formula: 1/Req = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + 1/R3. Given R1= 2Ω, R2= 4Ω, and R3 = 6Ω, we calculate:1/Req = 1/2 + 1/4 + 1/6 = 6/12 + 3/12 + 2/12 = 11/12Taking the reciprocal gives us Req = 12/11Ω. This makes Option C correct.Option A (12Ω) is incorrect because it assumes a series connection, not parallel. Option B (11/12Ω) is the reciprocal of the correct result, thus incorrect. Option D (None) is incorrect because there is a definitive equivalent resistance value.

questions.text: The resistivity of a wire is _ ohm-m if 0.75 A current flows through it by applying 1.5 V potential difference, take length and cross-sectional area as 5m and 2.5 x 10^-7 m^2.
questions.subject: Physics

For resistivity , first we have to find out resistanceV = I RSo R = V / IR = 1.5 / 0.75R = 2 ohmsR = l / A = resistivity length / area = R A / l = 2 2.5 x 10-7 / 5 = 1 x 10-7 ohm-m
For resistivity , first we have to find out resistanceV = I RSo R = V / IR = 1.5 / 0.75R = 2 ohmsR = l / A = resistivity length / area = R A / l = 2 2.5 x 10-7 / 5 = 1 x 10-7 ohm-m
For resistivity , first we have to find out resistanceV = I RSo R = V / IR = 1.5 / 0.75R = 2 ohmsR = l / A = resistivity length / area = R A / l = 2 2.5 x 10-7 / 5 = 1 x 10-7 ohm-m

questions.explanation: For resistivity , first we have to find out resistanceV = I RSo R = V / IR = 1.5 / 0.75R = 2 ohmsR = l / A = resistivity length / area = R A / l = 2 2.5 x 10-7 / 5 = 1 x 10-7 ohm-m

questions.text: Kirchhoff's first law is manifestation of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This law states that the total linear momentum of an isolated system remains constant if no external forces act on it. Kirchhoff's first law is not related to the conservation of momentum; it primarily deals with the conservation of electric charge in a circuit.

This law states that the mass of a closed system remains constant over time. Kirchhoff's first law is not related to the conservation of mass; it specifically addresses the conservation of electric charge in electrical circuits.

This law states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another. Kirchhoff's first law is not directly related to the conservation of energy; instead, it deals with the conservation of charge.

Kirchhoff's first law, also known as Kirchhoff's current law, is based on the principle of the conservation of electric charge. It states that the total electric current entering a junction in a circuit is equal to the total current leaving the junction. In other words, electric charge is conserved in a closed circuit.

questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's first law states that the algebraic sum of current flowing into a junction is zero. This rule corresponds to conservation of charge which states that total charge in a closed system remains constant because current is the rate of flow of charge.

questions.text: Three resistors are arranged as shown. The potential of the point O is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Applying KCL at point O allows us to determine that the potential is 3 V.
This option is incorrect. A calculation using KCL suggests that the potential is higher than 1 V.
This option is incorrect. A correct application of KCL results in a potential lower than 4 V.
This option is incorrect. The potential calculated using KCL is less than 5 V, indicating this choice is too high.

questions.explanation: To find the potential at point O, we apply Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL), which states that the sum of currents entering a junction equals the sum of currents leaving the junction. Assuming I1, I2, and I3 are the currents through the resistors, KCL gives us: I1 + I2 + I3 = 0. Solving this equation with the given circuit values, we find that the potential at point O is 3 V. Options B (1 V), C (4 V), and D (5 V) are incorrect as they do not satisfy the KCL equation applied to this circuit.

questions.text: Kirchhoff’s current law is applicable to which type of circuits:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a series circuit, the current remains the same throughout, but Kirchhoff's current law specifically applies to junctions where currents split or combine.
In parallel circuits, Kirchhoff's current law is critical for understanding how current divides among different branches, but it is not limited to this type of circuit.
This is the correct answer because Kirchhoff's current law applies universally to all types of circuits, including series, parallel, or any combination thereof.
This option is incorrect because Kirchhoff's current law does apply to circuit configurations, contradicting the claim that none of the types are applicable.

questions.explanation: Kirchhoff's current law states that the total current entering a junction must equal the total current leaving that junction. This law is applicable to all types of circuits, whether they are series, parallel, or a combination of both. In a series circuit, the current is the same at all points, and in a parallel circuit, it divides among the branches, but the law still holds true in both cases. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Any of these.' The other options are incorrect as they either limit the applicability of the law or incorrectly suggest it doesn't apply at all.

questions.text: A potentiometer is used to measure the emf of a cell. At null point, no current flows through _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Even at the null point, the main circuit carries current to maintain the potential difference across the potentiometer wire, which is essential for the device's operation.

At the null point, the potential difference across the length of the wire is equal to the emf of the cell, so there is no potential difference across the galvanometer. Therefore, no current flows through the galvanometer, and we can conclude that no current flows through the cell circuit at the null point. However, there is a current flowing through the main circuit (the circuit that includes the battery or power supply), as this is what is providing the potential difference across the wire in the potentiometer.

options.text: Both the main and cell circuits
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. While no current flows through the cell circuit at the null point, the main circuit still carries current to ensure the potentiometer functions correctly.
This option is incorrect because current must flow through the potentiometer wire as part of the main circuit to establish the necessary potential difference for the measurement.

questions.explanation: At the null point of a potentiometer, the potential difference across the potentiometer wire is equal to the emf of the cell being measured. This results in zero potential difference across the galvanometer, meaning no current flows through the cell circuit. However, the main circuit, including the potentiometer wire, continues to carry current to maintain the potential difference across the potentiometer wire. Therefore, the correct answer is that no current flows through the cell circuit, while the main circuit remains active to facilitate the measurement. Option B is correct, as it accurately describes the condition at the null point. Options A, C, and D are incorrect because they suggest that no current flows through the main circuit or potentiometer wire, which is necessary for the potentiometer's operation.

questions.text: In order to achieve high accuracy, the slide wire of a potentiometer should be
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Neither too small not too large

questions.explanation: Option A is correct since the slide wire of a potentiometer should be as long as possible for high accuracy so that we have a greater length for the current in the galvanometer to be zero if the potential drop across the standard voltage is too great to be measured. A long wire will ensure high accuracy since that length of the wire would be equal to the voltage or emf of the cell that you were trying to measure Option B is incorrect since the slide wire of the potentiometer should not be short for high accuracy since the short wire would not allow for greater readings if the voltage drop across the standard cell is too great for comparison with the source cell Option C is incorrect since a medium-sized wire would not allow for greater value readings too Option D is incorrect since a thick wire will also not allow for greater value readings. It will only decrease the resistance of the slide wire.

questions.text: Basically a potentiometer is a device for:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The potentiometer is a simple device used to measure the electrical potentials (or compare the e.m.f. of a cell). By comparing the potential at points along the wire with an unknown potential, the magnitude of the unknown potential can be determined

While potentiometers are not typically used directly for measuring current, they can indirectly measure current by converting it into a voltage. This is often done by placing a known resistance in series with the load and measuring the voltage drop across it using the potentiometer. However, the primary function of the potentiometer is not to directly measure current.

While it's technically possible to compare two currents using a potentiometer by converting the currents into voltages and then comparing the voltages, this is not a typical or direct application of a potentiometer.

Potentiometers are designed to measure voltage by providing a variable output voltage based on the position of a sliding contact along a resistive element. By connecting the potentiometer across a voltage source or circuit, the output voltage can be adjusted and measured.

questions.explanation: The potentiometer is a simple device used to measure the electrical potentials (or compare the e.m.f. of a cell). By comparing the potential at points along the wire with an unknown potential, the magnitude of the unknown potential can be determined.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a disadvantage of a potentiometer over a voltmeter?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: It can measure the internal resistance of a cell
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because a potentiometer cannot measure the internal resistance of a cell directly; it can only measure the potential difference across it.

options.text: It can measure the e.m.f. of a cell
options.explanation: Option B is incorrect because a potentiometer can measure the e.m.f. (electromotive force) of a cell by comparing it to a known standard cell.

options.text: It is heavy and not portable
options.explanation: C is correct as the disadvantage of a Potentiometer is its weight. It is very heavy and not portable. So mostly Voltmeter is used to measure Potential Difference.

options.text: It can measure accurately very small PD. of the ord of a few microvolts
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect because a potentiometer is capable of measuring accurately very small potential differences, even down to fractions of a microvolt.

questions.explanation: Options A, B, and D would rather be the advantages or uses of potentiometers, hence considering all the options, option C would be the disadvantage of potentiometers as potentiometers are, in fact, "heavy and not portable" compared to voltmeters.

questions.text: The sensitivity of a potentiometer can be Increased by _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Increasing the e.m.f. of the primary coil
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.
As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

options.text: increasing the length of the potentiometer wire
options.explanation: The sensitivity of a potentiometer can be increased by increasing the length of the potentiometer wire.The sensitivity of a potentiometer refers to the change in output voltage per unit length of the potentiometer wire. The increased length of the potentiometer wire allows for finer adjustment of the output voltage. It increases the resolution or sensitivity of the potentiometer, enabling more precise control or measurement of the voltage. Hence, this option is correct.

options.text: Decreasing the length of the potentiometer wire
options.explanation: As per the explanation, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The sensitivity of a potentiometer can be increased by increasing the length of the potentiometer wire.The sensitivity of a potentiometer refers to the change in output voltage per unit length of the potentiometer wire. The increased length of the potentiometer wire allows for finer adjustment of the output voltage. It increases the resolution or sensitivity of the potentiometer, enabling more precise control or measurement of the voltage.

questions.text: If the length of a potentiometer wire is doubled, the accuracy in determining the null point _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A potentiometer is a device used to measure potential difference (voltage) by comparing it with a known voltage. It consists of a uniform resistance wire, a galvanometer, and a sliding contact called the "jockey." The null point is the position on the wire where there is no potential difference between the two ends.When the length of the potentiometer wire is doubled, the overall length of the wire increases. This allows for a finer resolution and more precise measurements. The increased length provides a larger range for the sliding contact to be adjusted, making it easier to find the exact null point. Therefore, the accuracy in determining the null point is increased.
This option is incorrect because increasing the length of the wire actually improves the accuracy, not decreases it. A longer wire allows for finer measurement increments.
This is incorrect because the accuracy in determining the null point improves with the increased length of the potentiometer wire, contrary to the suggestion that it remains constant.
This option is incorrect because doubling the length of the wire consistently results in increased accuracy due to finer resolution, rather than it varying unpredictably.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct: Doubling the length of the potentiometer wire enhances the accuracy in determining the null point. The longer wire provides a greater range for the sliding contact (jockey) to move, allowing for more precise measurement and better resolution. This makes it easier to detect the exact point where the potential difference is zero.Options B, C, and D are incorrect: Increasing the length of the wire does not decrease or leave the accuracy unchanged, nor does it unpredictably change. The increased length consistently improves measurement precision, making these options inaccurate.

questions.text: Which of the following work (s) on the principle of wheatstone Bridge?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slide-wire bridge is a type of Wheatstone Bridge. It uses a sliding contact along a wire to find the balance point, allowing for the calculation of unknown resistances based on the bridge balance principle.

The meter bridge is a practical application of the Wheatstone Bridge. It uses a uniform wire, typically one meter long, to measure unknown resistances by finding a null point where the bridge is balanced.
A post office box is a refined version of the Wheatstone Bridge, employing precision resistors and a galvanometer to measure unknown resistances accurately through the bridge balance principle.

All the devices mentioned - slide-wire bridge, meter bridge, and post office box - operate on the principle of the Wheatstone Bridge, using the concept of balanced resistances to determine unknown values.
This option is incorrect as all of the mentioned devices function based on the Wheatstone Bridge principle.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'All of these'. Each option - slide-wire bridge, meter bridge, and post office box - utilizes the Wheatstone Bridge principle. This principle involves balancing the ratios of resistances to find unknown values, which is a common feature in all these devices. The 'None of these' option is incorrect because it contradicts the shared underlying principle of these devices.

questions.text: When we are measuring the internal resistance of a cell by potentiometer, the emf of the battery must be greater than the :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct option. The emf of the battery in the potentiometer must be greater than the emf of the cell to be measured to ensure the potential difference across the potentiometer wire can balance the cell's emf.
This option is incorrect. The potential difference (P.D) in the circuit is not relevant to the requirement that the battery's emf must exceed the cell's emf for accurate measurement.
This option is incorrect. The current in the cell does not directly influence the need for a higher battery emf in the potentiometer setup.
This option is incorrect. The current in the circuit is not directly related to the condition that the battery's emf should be higher than the cell's emf when using a potentiometer.

questions.explanation: The emf of the battery in the potentiometer experiment should be greater than the emf of the cell to be determined because The fall of potential across the potentiometer wire should not be less than the emf of the cell to be determined. And the potential across the wire is determined by the emf of the battery. If either the emf of the battery or the potential difference across the whole potentiometer wire is lesser than the emf of the experimental cell, the balance point will not be obtained. The balance point indicates that the emf of the cell to be determined is just balanced by the voltage drop that appears across the wire.

questions.text: When we are measuring the internal resistance of a cell by potentiometer, the emf of the battery must be greater than the :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct option. The emf of the battery in the potentiometer must be greater than the emf of the cell to be measured to ensure the potential difference across the potentiometer wire can balance the cell's emf.
This option is incorrect. The potential difference (P.D) in the circuit is not relevant to the requirement that the battery's emf must exceed the cell's emf for accurate measurement.
This option is incorrect. The current in the cell does not directly influence the need for a higher battery emf in the potentiometer setup.
This option is incorrect. The current in the circuit is not directly related to the condition that the battery's emf should be higher than the cell's emf when using a potentiometer.

questions.explanation: The emf of the battery in the potentiometer experiment should be greater than the emf of the cell to be determined because The fall of potential across the potentiometer wire should not be less than the emf of the cell to be determined. And the potential across the wire is determined by the emf of the battery. If either the emf of the battery or the potential difference across the whole potentiometer wire is lesser than the emf of the experimental cell, the balance point will not be obtained. The balance point indicates that the emf of the cell to be determined is just balanced by the voltage drop that appears across the wire.

questions.text: The instrument(s) which work on the principle of Wheatstone bridge is/are:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Meter bridge : an instrument that is used to find the unknown resistance of a coil or any other material. This bridge works under the principle of Wheatstone bridge.

The post office box is a pattern employed for examining the characteristics in an electric path. It is similar to a Wheatstone bridge of an electric path. It is applied for the calculation of resistance that is not known.

Carey Foster Bridge: a bridge based on the principle of Wheat stone's bridge that is used to compare two nearly equal resistances and to determine values of low resistances and the specific resistance of a wire.
Callender & Griffiths's bridge is used to measure unknown value of resistance. It is also used to measure the resistance of platinum resistance thermometers.

questions.explanation: All of the above mentioned instruments work upon the principle of Wheatstone bridge to measure the resistance except calendar.The Wheatstone bridge works on the principle of null deflection, i.e. the ratio of their resistances is equal, and no current flows through the circuit. Under normal conditions, the bridge is in an unbalanced condition where current flows through the galvanometer.All of the given examples work on this principle.

questions.text: In the given circuit, the galvanometer G will show zero deflection if :
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This equation does not represent the condition for a balanced bridge in this context.
This does not match the Wheatstone bridge balance condition and will not ensure zero current through the galvanometer.

The given circuit resembles a Wheatstone bridge, which is a configuration used to measure unknown resistances or to create a balanced circuit condition.In a balanced Wheatstone bridge, the galvanometer GG shows zero deflection when the ratio of the two pairs of resistors is equal, i.e.,R1/R2 = R3/R4Multiplying both sides gives:R1R4 = R2R3So, this is the correct condition for zero deflection in the galvanometer.
One of the options is indeed correct, making this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: The given circuit resembles a Wheatstone bridge, which is a configuration used to measure unknown resistances or to create a balanced circuit condition. In a balanced Wheatstone bridge, the galvanometer GG shows zero deflection when the ratio of the two pairs of resistors is equal, i.e.,R1/R2 = R3/R4Multiplying both sides gives:R1R4 = R2R3So, this is the correct condition for zero deflection in the galvanometer.

questions.text: The figure shows a meter bridge circuit, X = 12 Ω and R = 18 Ω. The jockey J is in the position of balance. If R is made 8Ω, through what distance will the jockey J have to be moved to obtain balance?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The balance point shifts more than this distance when R is changed to 8 Ω.

X=12ΩInitial R=18 ΩNew R=8 Meter bridge length = 100 cmLet the distance AJ be y1 before shifting and y2 after shifting.Before shifting:12/y1 = 18/100-y₁ => y₁ = 40cmAfter shifting: 12/y2 = 8/100-y₂ => y2 = 60 cmTherefore distance moved = 60–40 = 20 cmHence, this option is correct.
This option is incorrect. The shift is less than 30 cm. Detailed calculations show the shift is 20 cm.
This option is incorrect. A shift of 40 cm would imply a more significant change in resistance or a different initial setup.

questions.explanation: To solve this problem, we use the principle of a meter bridge, which states that at balance, the ratio of resistances is equal to the ratio of the lengths of the wire segments. Initially, when R = 18 Ω, we have:12/y1 = 18/(100-y₁) → y₁ = 40 cm.When R is changed to 8 Ω, the new balance equation is:12/y2 = 8/(100-y₂) → y₂ = 60 cm.Therefore, the jockey J has to be moved a distance of y₂ - y₁ = 60 cm - 40 cm = 20 cm to regain balance. The other options represent incorrect calculations of the balance shift and do not match the conditions specified by the problem statement.

questions.explanation: All of the above mentioned instruments work upon the principle of Wheatstone bridge to measure the resistance except calendar.The Wheatstone bridge works on the principle of null deflection, i.e. the ratio of their resistances is equal, and no current flows through the circuit. Under normal conditions, the bridge is in an unbalanced condition where current flows through the galvanometer.All of the given examples work on this principle.

questions.text: Electric power is?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Rate of electric work done per unit time
options.explanation: This is the correct definition of electric power. It represents how quickly energy is transferred or converted by an electric circuit. Mathematically, power (P) is the product of voltage (V) and current (I): P = VI.

This is incorrect. Voltage is the potential difference between two points in a circuit and does not directly measure power. Power involves both voltage and current, not just the rate of voltage change.
While electric charge per unit time relates to current (I), it does not define power. Power requires both current and voltage to describe the rate of energy transfer.
This is incorrect because current per unit time does not define power. Current is the rate of flow of charge, and power involves the product of current and voltage.

questions.explanation: Electric power is defined as the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred in an electric circuit. It is calculated as the product of voltage and current (P = VI). Option C incorrectly describes current, which is charge per unit time, not power. Option D is incorrect as it describes a rate of change in current, not power, which is a function of both voltage and current.

questions.text: Terminal potential difference of a cell.
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Increases with increase in its internal resistance
options.explanation: a) Increases with an increase in its internal resistance: The terminal potential difference of a cell is the voltage measured across its terminals when it is connected to an external circuit. When the internal resistance of the cell increases, it causes a voltage drop within the cell itself. As a result, the terminal potential difference decreases. Therefore, this option is not correct.

options.text: Decrease with an increase in internal resistance
options.explanation: b) Decreases with an increase in internal resistance: As explained above, an increase in internal resistance leads to a voltage drop within the cell, which results in a decrease in the terminal potential difference. So, this option is correct.

options.text: Is independent of its internal resistance
options.explanation: c) Is independent of its internal resistance: This statement is not true. The terminal potential difference is affected by the internal resistance of the cell, as explained in the previous options. Therefore, this option is not correct.
d) None of these: This option is incorrect because option (b) is correct. The terminal potential difference decreases with an increase in internal resistance.

questions.explanation: We know that terminal potential difference V = E -IrBy increasing the internal resistance, Ir factor increases which causes decrease in terminal potential differenceOption A,C and D are incorrect

questions.text: In a parallel circuit, the voltage remains the _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: In a parallel circuit, the voltage across each branch of the circuit is the same as the voltage across the voltage source. This is because each branch is connected directly across the voltage source, allowing the voltage to be the same for all branches. Therefore, the voltage remains the same in a parallel circuit.

questions.text: Our system stability is least affected by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The reactance of the generator is a measure of how much opposition it offers to the flow of alternating current.
The input torque is the amount of torque that is applied to the generator shaft.

System stability refers to the ability of a system to maintain its equilibrium or desired state of operation despite disturbances. Losses in a system, such as energy losses due to friction or resistance, have minimal impact on system stability. These losses do not significantly affect the overall behavior or performance of the system.
The reactance of the transmission line is a measure of how much opposition it offers to the flow of alternating current through the line.

questions.explanation: System stability refers to the ability of a system to maintain its equilibrium or desired state of operation despite disturbances. The system stability is least affected by the losses.The losses in a power system are the amount of power that is converted into heat and other forms of energy that are not useful. These losses can occur in the generators, transformers, transmission lines, and other components of the system.The losses in the system are not directly related to the stability of the system. The losses can affect the stability of the system indirectly by reducing the amount of power available to the system. However, the losses themselves do not directly affect the stability of the system.

Metal does not have both metallic and non-metallic characteristics , this option is incorrect.
Non-metal does not have both metallic and non-metallic characteristics , this option is incorrect.
Organic compound does not have both metallic and non-metallic characteristics , this option is incorrect.

questions.text: EMF becomes equal to the terminal potential difference when the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. When the circuit is closed, current flows, and the terminal potential difference is less than the EMF due to the voltage drop across the internal resistance of the battery.
This option is incorrect. When the current is maximum, the terminal potential difference is significantly less than the EMF due to the higher voltage drop across the internal resistance.

EMF is equal to the terminal potential difference when no current flows i.e. the circuit is open. The EMF (electromotive force) is the potential difference between the terminals of a battery when no current is flowing through an external circuit when the circuit is open.Terminal potential difference is the voltage across the terminals of the battery when the current is being drawn from it. The potential difference between the terminals of a battery will equal the EMF of the battery when there is no current in the battery. At this time the potential drop across the internal resistance is 0.

options.text: All of these options are correct
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because only the condition where the circuit is open results in the EMF being equal to the terminal potential difference.

questions.explanation: The EMF (electromotive force) represents the potential difference across the terminals of a battery when no current is flowing. When a circuit is open, no current flows, eliminating any voltage drop across the internal resistance of the battery, making the terminal potential difference equal to the EMF. In contrast, when the circuit is closed or the current is at its maximum, there is a non-zero current, resulting in a voltage drop across the internal resistance. This makes the terminal potential difference less than the EMF, invalidating options A and B. Therefore, option C is the only correct choice.

questions.text: The circuit which gives continuously varying potential is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A complex network refers to a circuit that consists of multiple interconnected components, such as resistors, capacitors, and/or inductors. While a complex network may contain components that contribute to varying potential, the term itself does not specifically denote a circuit that gives continuously varying potential.

A Wheatstone bridge is a specific configuration of resistive elements that is commonly used for measuring unknown resistances or for detecting small changes in resistance. It typically does not provide continuously varying potential; instead, it is used to balance or nullify a voltage difference.

A potential divider, also known as a voltage divider, is a circuit arrangement composed of resistive elements connected in series. It is designed to divide a voltage into smaller fractions across different points of the circuit. By adjusting the values of the resistors, the output voltage at the junction between the resistors can be continuously varied. This configuration indeed provides continuously varying potential.

options.text: All of the above options are correct
options.explanation: This option suggests that all the mentioned options are correct in describing a circuit that gives continuously varying potential. However, only one of the options, "Potential divider," accurately describes a circuit that achieves this function.

questions.explanation: A potential divider, also known as a voltage divider, is a circuit arrangement composed of resistive elements connected in series. It is designed to divide a voltage into smaller fractions across different points of the circuit. By adjusting the values of the resistors, the output voltage at the junction between the resistors can be continuously varied. This configuration indeed provides continuously varying potential.

questions.explanation: One microvolt is equal to 1/1,000,000 of a volt, which is the potential difference that would move one ampere of current against one ohm of resistance. The microvolt is a multiple of the volt, which is the SI derived unit for voltage. In the metric system, "micro" is the prefix for 10-6.

questions.text: Maximum power delivered by battery is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: It states that the output power of a source of current is maximum, when the internal resistance of the source is equal to the external resistance in the circuit. So, if R is the external resistance of the circuit and r is the internal resistance of the source of current (i.e. a battery) then the output power is maximum, when R = r.This theorem is applicable to all types of source of e.m.f. and is related with the output power and NOT with the power dissipated.If E is the applied e.m.f. of the source of e.m.f. i.e. a battery of internal resistance r and R is the external resistance then current in the circuit is given as:I = E/(R + r)At the maximum output power, R = r, so we have:I = E/(r + r) = E/(2r) and maximum output power:Pmax = I2r = E2/(4r)
Pmax = 4rE²: This formula is incorrect. The correct relationship involves the square of the voltage (E²) divided by a factor related to the internal resistance (r).
Pmax = VIT: This formula represents the general power equation (Power = Voltage x Current) but doesn't specifically address the condition for maximum power transfer from a battery.
Unlimited: The power delivered by a battery is limited by its internal resistance and voltage. It cannot deliver unlimited power.

questions.explanation: It states that the output power of a source of current is maximum, when the internal resistance of the source is equal to the external resistance in the circuit. So, if R is the external resistance of the circuit and r is the internal resistance of the source of current (i.e. a battery) then the output power is maximum, when R = r.This theorem is applicable to all types of source of e.m.f. and is related with the output power and NOT with the power dissipated.If E is the applied e.m.f. of the source of e.m.f. i.e. a battery of internal resistance r and R is the external resistance then current in the circuit is given as:I = E/(R + r)At the maximum output power, R = r, so we have:I = E/(r + r) = E/(2r) and maximum output power:Pmax = I2r = E2/(4r)

Power is the rate at which energy is consumed or produced. It is typically measured in watts (W)

The voltage, measured in volts (V), represents the electrical potential difference between two points in a circuit. Voltage alone does not measure energy, but rather the force or pressure that drives the electric current. It is not a direct measure of energy.

questions.explanation: A watt-hour (Wh) is a unit of energy; it's a way to measure the amount of work performed or generated. Household appliances and other electrical devices perform work,” which requires energy in the form of electricity.

questions.text: Two electric bulbs have 40W and 60W rating at 220V, then the ratio of their resistance will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. The resistance of a bulb is inversely proportional to its power rating when voltage is constant. Thus, the ratio of their resistances R2/R1 is the inverse of their power ratio P1/P2, leading to 3:2.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 3 : 2. The resistance of an electrical device is calculated using the formula R = V2/P, where R is resistance, V is voltage, and P is power. For the given bulbs:For the 40W bulb, R1 = 2202/40.For the 60W bulb, R2 = 2202/60.Thus, the ratio of their resistances R2/R1 = (2202/60) / (2202/40) = 40/60 = 2/3. Therefore, R1/R2 = 3/2, which simplifies to a resistance ratio of 3 : 2.The other options are incorrect because they do not reflect the inverse relationship between power and resistance at a constant voltage.

questions.text: If a wire is moulded in the form of a ring of radius r which encloses a charge q, then the strength of the electric field at the centre of the ring will be
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a wire is molded in the form of a ring and carries a charge q, the electric field created by the ring at any point on its axis is given by the equation: E = (k * q) / (r^2 + x^2)^(3/2) Where: E is the electric field strength, k is the electrostatic constant, q is the charge enclosed by the ring, r is the radius of the ring, and x is the distance along the axis from the center of the ring. To find the electric field at the center of the ring, we set x = 0. Plugging this value into the equation, we get: E = (k * q) / (r^2 + 0^2)^(3/2) E = (k * q) / r^3 Since the electric field is inversely proportional to r^3, as r approaches 0, the electric field strength approaches infinity. However, at the exact center of the ring, r is 0, which means the electric field strength becomes undefined or infinite. Therefore, the correct answer is A. The strength of the electric field at the center of the ring is 0 because the formula for the electric field becomes undefined at that point.
This is incorrect. A non-zero value implies an imbalance in the electric field at the center, which is not possible in a symmetrical ring.
This is incorrect. A value of 2 suggests a significant field strength, which cannot occur at the center of a symmetrically charged ring.
This is incorrect. Like other non-zero options, it implies a non-existent asymmetry in the field due to symmetrical charge distribution.

questions.explanation: When a wire is molded in the form of a ring and carries a charge q, the electric field created by the ring at any point on its axis is given by the equation: E = (k * q) / (r^2 + x^2)^(3/2) Where: E is the electric field strength, k is the electrostatic constant, q is the charge enclosed by the ring, r is the radius of the ring, and x is the distance along the axis from the center of the ring. To find the electric field at the center of the ring, we set x = 0. Plugging this value into the equation, we get: E = (k * q) / (r^2 + 0^2)^(3/2) E = (k * q) / r^3 Since the electric field is inversely proportional to r^3, as r approaches 0, the electric field strength approaches infinity. However, at the exact center of the ring, r is 0, which means the electric field strength becomes undefined or infinite. Therefore, the correct answer is A. The strength of the electric field at the center of the ring is 0 because the formula for the electric field becomes undefined at that point.

questions.text: As compared to thin wires, thick wires have?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Thin wires offer more resistance than thick wires. The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area. Thicker wires have a larger cross-sectional area, which means that they offer less resistance to the flow of electric current compared to thinner wires. This is because the larger cross-sectional area provides more space for the electrons to move, reducing the likelihood of collisions and increasing the flow of current. Therefore, thick wires have less resistance than thin wires and can carry more current without overheating or losing energy due to resistance.

Thick wires do have resistance, but they offer less resistance compared to thin wires. The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area. Thicker wires have a larger cross-sectional area, which means that they offer less resistance to the flow of electric current compared to thinner wires. This is because the larger cross-sectional area provides more space for the electrons to move, reducing the likelihood of collisions and increasing the flow of current. Therefore, thick wires have less resistance than thin wires and can carry more current without overheating or losing energy due to resistance. However, thick wires still have some resistance, which depends on their length, temperature, and the material they are made of.

R = p L/A Thick wires have less resistance than thin wires because of their greater cross-sectional area. The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area, as described by Ohm's law. Thicker wires have a larger cross-sectional area, which means that they offer less resistance to the flow of electric current compared to thinner wires. This is because the larger cross-sectional area provides more space for the electrons to move, reducing the likelihood of collisions and increasing the flow of current. Therefore, thick wires have less resistance than thin wires and can carry more current without overheating or losing energy due to resistance.

Thick and thin wires do not have the same resistances. The resistance of a wire is directly proportional to its length and inversely proportional to its cross-sectional area, as described by Ohm's law. Thicker wires have a larger cross-sectional area, which means that they offer less resistance to the flow of electric current compared to thinner wires. This is because the larger cross-sectional area provides more space for the electrons to move, reducing the likelihood of collisions and increasing the flow of current. Therefore, thick wires have less resistance than thin wires and can carry more current without overheating or losing energy due to resistance. The resistance of a wire also depends on its length, temperature, and the material it is made of.

questions.text: If four different resistors are used to pass electric current for different time durations, then which of the following will produce the largest heating effect in joules?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: When 1 amp current is passed through 3 ohm resistor for 2 sec.
options.explanation: To determine the largest heating effect in joules, we need to calculate the heat produced in each scenario using the formula: Heat (H) = I^2 * R * t, where I is the current in amperes, R is the resistance in ohms, and t is the time in seconds.a. When 1 amp current is passed through 3 ohm resistor for 2 sec.H = (1^2) * 3 * 2 = 6 joulesOption A results in the same heating effect of 6 joules, which is smaller than the heating effect in option D.

options.text: When 1 amp current is passed through 2 ohm resistor for 3 sec.
options.explanation: To determine the largest heating effect in joules, we need to calculate the heat produced in each scenario using the formula: Heat (H) = I^2 * R * t, where I is the current in amperes, R is the resistance in ohms, and t is the time in seconds.b. When 1 amp current is passed through 2 ohm resistor for 3 sec.H = (1^2) * 2 * 3 = 6 joules Option B results in the same heating effect of 6 joules, which is smaller than the heating effect in option D.

options.text: When 2 amps current is passed through 1 ohm resistor for 2 sec.
options.explanation: To determine the largest heating effect in joules, we need to calculate the heat produced in each scenario using the formula: Heat (H) = I^2 * R * t, where I is the current in amperes, R is the resistance in ohms, and t is the time in seconds.c. When 2 amp current is passed through 1-ohm resistor for 2 sec.H = (2^2) * 1 * 2 = 8 joulesOption C produces a heating effect of 8 joules, which is smaller than the heating effect in option D.

options.text: When 3 amps current is passed through 1 ohm resistor for 1 sec.
options.explanation: To determine the largest heating effect in joules, we need to calculate the heat produced in each scenario using the formula: Heat (H) = I^2 * R * t, where I is the current in amperes, R is the resistance in ohms, and t is the time in seconds.d. When 3 amp current is passed through 1-ohm resistor for 1 sec.H = (3^2) * 1 * 1 = 9 joulesComparing the results, we can see that option D produces the largest heating effect with 9 joules.

questions.explanation: To determine the largest heating effect in joules, we use the formula H = I^2 * R * t for each scenario:Option A: H = (1^2) * 3 * 2 = 6 joules.Option B: H = (1^2) * 2 * 3 = 6 joules.Option C: H = (2^2) * 1 * 2 = 8 joules.Option D: H = (3^2) * 1 * 1 = 9 joules.Option D produces the largest heating effect of 9 joules. The other options produce less heat because either the current, resistance, or time duration is not as optimal for maximizing the heat produced.

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The power rating of the 50W bulb is lower than the other options. In the formula P= V^2/R, since the voltage (V) is constant for all bulbs, a lower power rating corresponds to a higher resistance (R). Therefore, the 50W bulb will have higher resistance compared to bulbs with higher power ratings. Hence, the 50W bulb has more resistance compared to the other options.

Similar to the 50W bulb, the 100W bulb has a lower power rating compared to the options with higher power ratings. Using the same reasoning, the 100W bulb will have higher resistance compared to bulbs with higher power ratings. Therefore, the 100W bulb has more resistance compared to the other options.

The 200W bulb has a higher power rating compared to the 50W and 100W bulbs but is lower than the 300W bulb. According to the formula P= V^2/R, a higher power rating corresponds to lower resistance (R). Therefore, the 200W bulb will have less resistance compared to the 50W and 100W bulbs but more resistance compared to the 300W bulb.

Among the given options, the 300W bulb has the highest power rating. As per the formula P= V^2/R, a higher power rating corresponds to lower resistance (R). Therefore, the 300W bulb will have the least resistance compared to the other options.
